text: Usually no , if they 're of similar ages . What you 're looking for are called " Romeo and Juliet laws " , which are exceptions to statutory rape laws . Rules vary by state and country . For example : In 2006 , the Canadian government passed a bill to raise the age of consent from 14 to 16 , while creating a close - in - age exemption for sex between 14 - 15 year olds and partners up to 5 years older , and keeping an existing close - in - age clause for sex between 12 - 13 year olds and partners up to 2 years older . So yes , two minors can have consensual sex without it being statutory rape . Depends on the state / country and their ages . Of importance to note , age of consent ( when one is considered old enough to legally decide to voluntarily have sex ) and age of majority ( age when one is no longer considered to be a minor , but instead is an adult , legally responsible for all of their behavior ) are often quite different .

Testosterone is a hormone in the body . Women and men have testosterone , but men have more of it . Testosterone causes men to be manly . Male pattern baldness is caused by one of the products of testosterone that is formed when testosterone breaks down . The medical word for male pattern baldness is called Androgenic alopecia , which means baldness ( alopecia ) caused by androgens ( male hormones ) . The product of testosterone that causes male pattern baldness is [ 5DHT ] ( URL_3 ) . It 's made by a special enzyme that is on some hair follicles - only on the scalp and not on the body . When 5DHT gets made , it binds to the hair follicles and then kills off the hair in that follicle . Not everyone has the same amount of the enzyme , so that 's why some men go bald and some do n't . [ There is an effective preventative measure of male pattern baldness ] ( URL_4 ) called castration . If you are castrated your testicles have been removed so you have much less testosterone . This also leads to bone thinning , fat gain , muscle loss and lots of other changes that make a man 's body less manly and more like that of a woman 's . Because testosterone makes men masculine but is also the thing that gets made into 5DHT it is very difficult to find something that will stop male pattern baldness without also effectively castrating the man . The solution would be to do with blocking 5DHT getting made but that requires messing around with receptor sites and stuff and is complicated and might be too expensive to research without lots and lots of financial backing . [ There are some medicines that might do this ] ( URL_1 ) but because of what they do , they can still cause bad things to happen like breast growth , lower sex drive and impotence . This is because 5DHT is [ used by the body for other things ] ( URL_0 ) - men born without the enzyme that makes it [ often get raised as girls ] ( URL_2 ) . So we ca n't cure male pattern baldness easily . It 's like trying to play jenga - if you pull out the " bald " block , you might also knock out the " healthy prostate " block and the " no breasts " block because they 're resting on the bald block . Mostly for some of the same reasons that we ca n't stop aging . We can slow it down , we can try to mask it but ultimately we can not stop it . It is a genetic condition rather than a disease so there is n't anything to ' kill ' that would fix the problem . Its possible that with gene therapies in the near future we will be able to do something about this , but adding segments of dna to people is something that has to be studied a LOT before anyone would be comfortable about doing something like that over a little hair loss . This is n't exactly ELI5 but I remember this question being posed in high school biology a few years back . Basically every man and woman has two sets of chromosomes , equally to a set number of pairs . Women have two X chromosomes ( thus engendering their … gender ) , and men have on X from the mother and one Y from the father . You may already see the problem here . If the woman passes along just one X to her son , there will be only one set of data that is " male " data ; this includes male pattern baldness . This is also why you see practically no examples of woman truly balding in old age because they have " back up " DNA ( obviously not the real terminology but we 're in ELI5 here ) . Men are generally doomed to be bald if the men in their mother 's family are also bald due to the lack of two capable X chromosomes . Hope that makes sense .

To the best of my knowledge , the power just keeps flowing and the states settle the bill between the power companies . I know for a fact that in Washington and California , the states have a pre - organized plan to share power with each other : Washington providing hydropower to California when they have an excess of it and when demand is high in CA ( such as summers with lots of AC running ) and California shares it in reverse when the flow of rivers like the Colombia river is lower during other parts of the year . To the power companies , there is no state or national line ( some companies are private , some are public ) . At least in north america , everyone uses 120V/60Hz power , so it 's physically regulated the same . They just monitor which way the current flows , and one side pays the other side . Depending on where you live , and who brought you the power , there may be different charges and fees . Some companies ca n't produce enough power , so they buy it ( usually at a much lower rate ) from another power producer . [ wiki link on electricity trade ] ( URL_0 ) To potential future answerers I think the OP was asking in regards to governmental regulations . Is there some sort of special system in place for towns that sits on state borders ? Lets say state A had a power plant and state B had strict regulations on power generation . Town C sits on the border of these states . Would the town run into any weird issues or would all power generated in state A only have to follow State A regulations ?

" First I am not a scientist or an expert . Second this is based off an argument by David Sloan Wilson , and has not been fully factually proven but is one hypothesis on why we laugh . So this might be too general , but from what I have learned is that laughter , or at least early forms of it were similar to alerting someone to a false alarm . Laughter signals to other humans that a situation that looks dangerous , or harmful , is not . So say someone slips and falls . If they hit there head and blood starts pouring out it is an emergency , and most people would not laugh . Now if someone slips and falls , and then gets up , there is not danger or emergency , and as a signal to other humans we laugh . Like many traits , today , laughter has lost its evolutionary importance , but in early development laughter was a quick , and widespread signal to other humans that a possibly dangerous situation , is in fact harmless . Most of this comes from : Evolution for Everybody by : David Sloan Wilson . " < - from a thread just above this one I do n't . And I 've never understood why others do . In the case of the stooges , it was funny usually not THAT they were hurt , but the entirety of the situation that led to a stooge getting hurt , usually due to a blunder on another stooge 's part .

It's important to note that there is not necessarily a single reason why someone might believe that all drugs should be legalized. Different people might have different reasons for supporting the legalization of all drugs. Here are a few potential reasons that someone might support the legalization of all drugs:

Personal freedom: Some people believe that adults should be free to make their own decisions about what they put into their bodies, as long as they are not harming others. They might argue that the government should not have the power to prohibit people from using drugs if they choose to do so.

Health and safety: Some people believe that drugs are safer when they are regulated and controlled by the government. They might argue that drugs like methamphetamine are currently produced in illegal labs, which can be dangerous and result in impure or contaminated products. Legalizing and regulating drugs could make them safer for people to use.

Reducing crime: Some people believe that the current laws against drugs create a black market for drugs, which can lead to criminal activity and violence. They might argue that legalizing drugs would reduce crime by taking away the illegal market for drugs.

It's important to note that these are just a few potential reasons why someone might support the legalization of all drugs. There are also many valid arguments against the legalization of all drugs, and the issue is complex and controversial.

Salt water works in three ways . First , it dehydrates the bacteria due to osmosis - that is , the water in the bacteria gets " sucked " across the cell membrane into your salt solution due to the concentration differences of the solutions to the point where the bacteria is no longer functional , effectively killing it . Second , it serves to physically remove any of the bacteria from the wound , and is able to penetrate deep ( giggity ) into the wound to get any bacteria that may be lurking below the surface . Third , it also dehydrates your cells in the wound , effectively killing off the surface cells that have been damaged by the bacteria , and washes them away so that the cells underneath can be regenerated and heal more effectively . Any irritation that is caused simply causes inflammation to redirect the healing processes of your body towards the wound , which also contributes to the healing process . I was told this is because of both the action of ' rinsing ' ( which could be with just plain water ) that flushes out debris and bacteria , and osmosis . A sufficiently concentrated slat water solution has a lower concentration of water than the tissues in your mouth , so whilst rinsing it draws fluid from the tissues into the solution . This can help ' flush wounds ' because fluid is pushing out from the tissue . Also as it acts as an ' astringent ' this action can cause tissues to contract , and increase blood flow to the area . At high enough concentrations the osmotic effect can destroy some bacteria - but often at concentrations that actually harms the tissues of the mouth . Salt water aka normal saline is an isotonic solution which is closest to the tonicity with in your body ( fluids with in our body contain sodium as well as other electrolytes ) . It allows the removal of debris etc from the site that is healing , in turn promoting the proliferation and maturation ( skin healing and coming together ) stage .

A stock dividend converts some of the reserves and surplus on the company's balance sheet into paid-up capital and securities premium account without involving any actual cash outflow to the shareholders. While cash dividends are eyed by the investors due to their cash yield, issuance of stock dividends are indicators of growing confidence of the management and the shareholders in the company.  The fact that shareholders want to convert free cash sitting on the balance sheet (which can ideally be taken out as dividends) into blocked money in exchange for shares is symbolic to their confidence in the company. This in turn is expected to lead to an increase in market price of the stock.

It's natural to feel physical sensations in response to strong emotions, and heartache is no exception. When we feel emotionally distressed, our bodies can react in physical ways. In the case of heartache, it's common to feel a tightness or pain in the chest.

This physical sensation is caused by the body's natural stress response, which is often referred to as the "fight or flight" response. When we feel threatened or distressed, our bodies release stress hormones, such as adrenaline and cortisol. These hormones can cause physical symptoms, including an increased heart rate, shallow breathing, and tightness in the chest.

So, while heartache is an emotional experience, it can also have physical effects on the body. It's important to take care of yourself when you're feeling heartache, both emotionally and physically. This might include talking to a trusted friend or family member, participating in activities that bring you joy, and getting enough rest and exercise.

Yes, it is possible for business losses to offset your income on a joint tax return. If your wife has a business that is operated at a loss, that loss can be used to offset your income on a joint tax return. This can result in a lower overall tax liability for you and your wife.There are a few things to keep in mind when using business losses to offset your income on a joint tax return:The business must be a sole proprietorship or a partnership in which your wife is a partner. Business losses from corporations and other business entities cannot be used to offset your income on a joint tax return.The business loss must be "ordinary" and "necessary" in order to be deductible. Ordinary losses are those that are common and accepted in your trade or business. Necessary losses are those that are appropriate and helpful for the development of your trade or business.Your wife's business loss can only offset your income up to the amount of your taxable income for the year. If the loss is greater than your taxable income, the excess loss can be carried forward to future tax years.It is always a good idea to consult with a tax professional when it comes to using business losses to offset your income on a joint tax return. They can help you understand the rules and limitations and ensure that you are properly reporting your business losses on your tax return.

Nuclear fusion is a way to transform mass into energy by fusing atoms . In theory this method is known for a long time and if it works it could provide a clean , nearly unlimited and non - polluting source of energy . But in practice it 's very difficult to create energy this way . The experiment in Germany is making big steps of improvement towards the realization of this technology . You probably know Einsteins equation E = mc^2 . It says that if mass were to be converted into energy , it would be done with a factor of the speed of light * squared * . These are huge numbers ! So even if you are able to convert a small amount of mass , it will release very much energy . With nuclear fusion , the nuclei of two hydrogen atoms ( at least in this case , it can be done with helium as well ) are fused in superheated plasma . With this fusion , some mass is converted into energy . Plasma is an excited state of an atom . For instance the flame of a fire is a plasma , or the lightning in a thunderstorm . The fusion reactor creates such a plasma and it needs to be super hot . But if these temperatures hit the metal of the reactor , the reactor would melt ( we 're talking like a million degrees here ) . So how to contain a plasma that would melt anything it touches ? Contain it in a magnetic field . With magnets the size of a small car , a magnetic field is created in which the plasma can exist . These magnets are also cooled to near absolute zero temperatures to create super conductance . Just imagine the engineering that needs to be done to create a machine that can do this ? Many have tried and some have succeeded a little , but the W7 - X in Germany is so far the most promising . Although they were able to contain a plasma for only a very short time , this is a major leap forward into maturing this technology . The current reactor is a test setup and will never be used to create actual usable energy . But it 's helping us creating the power source of the future . [ Edit ] A nice guy / gal pointed out that I said " creating energy " , while of course I should have said " transformed " . Fusion power is a game changing technology if we can get it to work ; potentially limitless free energy . The stellarator in Germany is a new type of fusion reactor , and the experiment yesterday was the first time a confined hydrogen fusion process in a stellarator had been achieved . The easiest way to explain it is that all energy comes from the sun . Wind is from the sun heating the air unevenly . Hydro is from the sun evaporating a portion of the oceans to have it condense over mountains and flow through our turbines along rivers . Coal is from crushed plant matter which grew from sunlight . Oil is from crushed animal matter which grew from eating plants which grew from sunlight . Solar power is just our most direct way of gathering energy from our star . In space there would be zero resistance and you 'd be gathering energy very efficiently . Here on earth though , some of that solar energy is absorbed and defused by the atmosphere . Nuclear fusion is just creating a tiny sun here on earth and gathering that energy directly .

It 's like legos . End of story . There are three reasons people play , I 've found out . Usually people have a favorite of the three reasons . -Adventure : Some people like seeking out monsters , and are delighted when they find a cave underground , and spelunk with a sword and torches to light their way . They love finding natural beauty ( e.g. a lone tree on the peak of a tall hill ) . -Crafting : Some people like searching for exotic minerals in the ground , often creating elaborate mine shafts in order to search for the precious diamond . These people also like to farm and bake things like cake with materials they 've found . -Building : Definitely the most popular , some people like creating buildings for the sake of creation . They are the people who construct castles , dream homes , and complicated rail systems . Minecraft is like computer legos , after all . [ ZeroPunctuation 's video review ] ( URL_0 ) sums it up well . The gist of it is that rather than just being a sandbox game where you can do anything you like , it has challenges ( the need to gather building material and construct tools , monsters that harm you and destroy your creations ) that make your finished creations all the more rewarding . The multi - player collaborative building aspect is probably another reason for its success .

It is a rather complex system, but here is a rough summary. Interbank tranfers ultimately require a transfer of reserves at the central bank. As a concrete example, the bank of england system is the rtgs. Only the clearing banks and similar (e.g. bacs) have access to rtgs. You can send a chaps payment fairly quickly, but that costs. Chaps immediately triggers an rtgs transfer once the sending bank agrees and so you can be certain that the money is being paid.  Hence its use for large amounts. Bacs also sits on the rtgs but to keep costs down it batches tranfers up. Because we are talking about bank reserve movements, checks have to be in place and that can take time. Furthermore the potential for fraud is higher than chaps since these are aggregrated transactions a layer removed, so a delay reduces the chance of payment failing after apparently being sent. Faster payments is a new product by bacs that speeds up the bacs process by doing a number of transfers per day. Hence the two hour clearing. For safety it can only be used for up to 10k. Second tier banks will hold accounts with clearing banks so they are another step down. Foreign currency transfers require the foreign Central Bank reserve somewhere, and so must be mediated by at least one clearing bank in that country. Different countries are at different stages in their technology.  Uk clearing is 2h standard now but US is a little behind I believe.  Much of Europe is speeding up. Rather like bitcoin clearing, you have a choice between speed and safety. If you wait you are more certain the transaction is sound and have more time to bust the transfer.

Firstly , I 'm only answering OP 's question , I 'm not speaking as a proponent of conspiracy theories . Most of them are centered around the idea that 9/11 was an inside job carried out by our own government , and you will hear tons of supporting conspiracy theories looking to provide details backing up this notion . Supporting conspiracy theories in general fall into 3 main categories . 1 ) Prove a motive of an alternate party . In this case , many 9/11 theorists want to convince people that our government could have a reason for staging the WTC and pentagon attacks . 2 ) Disprove or discredit prominent existing theories . In this case , they want to make people less certain of the idea that 9/11 was caused by foreign terrorists who hate the US . 3 ) Suggest alternative theories . In this case , they present many alternate things that could have caused the destruction of those buildings other than the commonly accepted idea . These go hand in hand , you want to convince people that something else happened , so you need to show them how it could have happened differently , who could have done it and why , as well as convince them that the existing theory is less valid . Edit : examples of each : 1 ) The most common motive / suspect in these conspiracy theories is that the government wanted to justify a war on terror by causing a spectacle to rally the people against terrorists . 2 ) One theory points out that jet fuel ca n't burn hot enough to melt the steel in the WTC . As an aero / mechanical engineer working as a structural analyst , I am not even going to begin to comment on how fucking stupid this is , but people do say it . 3 ) There is a common theory that bursts of smoke / dust from the base of the building indicate some sort of controlled detonation . I 'll let you guys pick these apart and add other examples . I watched a movie on Netflix called " Zeitgeist " and the whole second act of the documentary was about some 9/11 conspiracies . It was really fascinating and rather convincing , even though I have a hard time believing in conspiracies otherwise . Anyway , watch that movie some time and you 'd be surprised . Simply put they want to blame the government / NWO / G.W. Bush jr . or anyone else for the attack . Almost after any sort of tragedy there are people who come out and say that it was really caused by some secret organization or the government was behind it . People do this because they want to believe that the only people who could have done this was a large nameless organization . They can also want to blame the current people in power for it because they are trying to control more of the country through fear . I believe it was the Newton school shooting there were people who said that it never happened and it was a ploy by the government to pass more restrictive gun laws . With 9/11 it was something that was n’t going to be forgotten after a week or so , it is still in people ’s minds . All the “ evidence ” i.e. the towers , has been hauled off so the only thing we have left are peoples first hand accounts of what happened and whatever video footage that is left . This makes it easy for people to say that the only way the towers fell were due to control demolition or that the planes were filled with explosives and remotely flown into the towers . The 9/11 conspiracies can be looked at from two broad points of view : 1 . The government knew about the attack and let it happen 2 . The government had a hand it planning / carrying out the attacks From here people can say that the towers fell due to : * Controlled demolition . * Thermite charges , another form of controlled demolition . * Planes filled with explosives . * Missiles were fired and planes did not hit the towers . This leads to why this has happened : * Invasions of Afghanistan and Iraq . * Expansion of the US military . * Expansion of US influence across the globe . * Greed / Money . I hope this answers your question .

Postmodern literature is literature characterized by heavy reliance on techniques like fragmentation, paradox, and questionable narrators, and is often (though not exclusively) defined as a style or trend which emerged in the post–World War II era.

Certainly! I'd be happy to give you a basic overview of how computers work. Computers are machines that can process and store information. They do this by using tiny electronic components called "transistors" to perform calculations and store data. Inside a computer, there is a central processing unit (CPU) that acts as the "brain" of the computer. The CPU is responsible for executing instructions and performing calculations. These instructions tell the computer what to do, such as open a program or save a file. Data is stored in a computer using a memory system. There are different types of memory, such as RAM (random access memory) and ROM (read-only memory), each with its own specific purpose. RAM is used to store data that the computer is currently using or processing, while ROM is used to store data that is permanently or semi-permanently written onto the memory, such as the computer's operating system. The computer also has input and output devices, which allow you to interact with it. Some common examples of input devices are keyboards, mice, and touchscreens, while examples of output devices include monitors and printers. Finally, computers are connected to each other and the Internet through a network of wires and wireless signals. This allows them to communicate and share information with each other. I hope this helps give you a basic understanding of how computers work! Let me know if you have any other questions.

Usenet is a system that allows users to post and read messages on various topics, called "newsgroups." It's kind of like a giant online discussion forum.

To use Usenet, you need a special program called a "newsreader." This lets you connect to a Usenet server and browse the different newsgroups that are available. You can then read the messages that other people have posted and respond to them by posting your own messages.

Usenet is organized into different newsgroups that cover different topics, such as technology, sports, and entertainment. You can choose which newsgroups you want to join and participate in the discussions.

To get started with Usenet, you'll need to find a newsreader program and a Usenet server to connect to. Some internet service providers (ISPs) offer Usenet access as part of their services, or you can use a third-party provider. Once you have those things set up, you can start exploring the different newsgroups and joining in on the discussions!

The United Nations (UN) is an international organization that was created after World War II to promote international cooperation and try to prevent conflicts between countries. The UN has many different bodies and agencies that work on a variety of issues, including peace and security, human rights, and development. One of the main ways that the UN tries to prevent conflict is by promoting diplomacy and dialogue between countries. This means that the UN tries to bring countries together to talk about their problems and find peaceful solutions. The UN also has a peacekeeping mission, which involves sending troops to areas where there is a risk of conflict or where a conflict has already occurred. The peacekeeping mission helps to monitor the situation and try to prevent further violence. Unfortunately, despite the best efforts of the UN, there are still conflicts and wars that occur around the world. The UN can't always prevent these things from happening, especially if one or more countries are not willing to cooperate or listen to the UN's recommendations. Airbags are designed to deploy in a collision or impact that meets certain threshold levels of force. The exact level of force required to trigger an airbag deployment can vary depending on the specific system and the type of sensor used.

Generally, airbags are designed to deploy in a front-end collision when the vehicle decelerates rapidly, such as in a crash or during a sudden stop. The deployment of the airbags is intended to provide additional protection for the vehicle's occupants by helping to cushion and absorb some of the impact energy.

It's important to note that airbags are not designed to deploy in every collision or impact, and there are certain situations in which they may not deploy even if the vehicle experiences a significant impact. For example, airbags may not deploy if the collision is a low-speed impact or if the vehicle is involved in a rollover accident. Airbags are designed to deploy in a collision that meets certain criteria, such as a certain amount of deceleration or a certain impact force. The specific criteria for deployment can vary depending on the make and model of the vehicle and the specific airbag system it is equipped with.

In general, airbags are designed to deploy in frontal and near-frontal collisions, as well as some side collisions and rollovers. When the vehicle's sensors detect a collision that meets the criteria for deployment, an electrical signal is sent to the airbag inflator, which causes a chemical reaction that rapidly generates a large volume of gas. This gas inflates the airbag, which is stored in a compact form within the steering wheel or dashboard. The airbag then deploys and inflates rapidly to cushion the impact and help protect the occupants of the vehicle.

It is important to note that airbags are not designed to deploy in all types of collisions, and deploying an airbag does not necessarily mean that the occupants of the vehicle will be protected from all injuries. Airbags are one element of a vehicle's overall safety system and are intended to work in conjunction with seat belts, which should always be worn, and other safety features such as crumple zones and rollover protection. The Sun is plasma . Plasma is one of the 4 phases of matter . It 's not solid , liquid , or gas . It 's the same state of matter that 's in a neon or flourescent light bulb or lightning bolt -- kinda gaseous but split into positive and negative particles . If you were immune to heat / fusion / damage , you could fall into the sun and eventually sort of float because you 'd reach a point in the inside of the sun where the plasma is denser than you . So like a hot air balloon , your insides would be less dense than your surroundings causing you to float . But if you were to keep going down , you 'd reach the center of the sun where nuclear fusion is taking place . And even though it 's not solid , the density is much higher than lead or uranium . Let 's first think about what it means to ' land ' on other surfaces . These are places which have human - scale sudden increases in particle density usually associated with materials in states ( e.g. gaseous atmosphere transitioning to the solid earth or the liquid ocean ) . Our best guess about the inside of the sun is that there is no such human - scale transition , but the core of the sun is ~150x as dense as water on Earth 's surface . This density increases as one descends through the solar ' atmosphere ' . The maximum density of solid materials on Earth 's surface is ~20x water density and any craft you take on this hypothetical journey will be less dense than this . Ignoring all of the terrible things which will kill you inside the sun from heat to crushing to massive flows of material , you would fall through an ever - thickening soup until you stopped sinking because you were n't dense enough to fall any farther . In this sense , you have ' landed ' , but it would n't be with a satisfying ' splash ' or ' thud ' which is what I think you would expect . ( Disclaimer : justchawk is not an Astrophysicist ) Before you 'd be able to land on the surface , you 'd have to contend with the ever - increasing Solar Radiation Pressure ; basically the photon density being so thick that it exerts a pressure on you : URL_0 I 'm not sure what this would be at the surface of the sun , but it increases with the inverse square of distance . If it 's high enough , you may experience what scientists refer to as " Walking on Sunshine . " Edit : Plugged in some numbers , looks like it would be less than 1 N / m^2 ; you 'll be fine .

Sure! Let's pretend that you have a cookie jar that represents your virtual currency, and each cookie in the jar represents one unit of your virtual currency. When you bought $200 worth of virtual currency, you were essentially buying a certain number of cookies from someone else. 
Now, imagine that the value of each cookie can change over time, just like the value of a real-world currency like the US dollar can change. If the value of each cookie goes up, then the total value of all the cookies in your jar will also go up. 
For example, let's say that when you bought your cookies, each one was worth $1. But over the next four years, the value of each cookie goes up to $10. If you have 200 cookies in your jar, then the total value of your cookies would be 200 cookies * $10 per cookie = $2,000. 
Now, let's say that the value of each cookie goes up even more, to $12. If you have 200 cookies in your jar, then the total value of your cookies would be 200 cookies * $12 per cookie = $2,400. 
So, if the value of each unit of your virtual currency goes up over time, then the total value of your virtual currency can also go up. That's how $200 worth of virtual currency can turn into $2.4 million in four years.

Instead, they rely on other means of protection, such as seeking shade or wearing protective clothing.

Railguns are a type of weapon that use electricity to launch a projectile at very high speeds. They have the potential to be very powerful and accurate, but there are several reasons why militaries have not yet adopted them for widespread use.

One reason is that railguns require a lot of electricity to operate. This means that they would need to be connected to a large power source, such as a generator, in order to function. This could make them difficult to use in certain situations, such as on a tank that is moving around on the battlefield.

Another reason is that railguns generate a lot of heat when they are used. This heat can damage the weapon over time, and could make it less reliable.

Finally, railguns are still being developed and tested, and there are many technical challenges that need to be overcome before they can be used in real-world military applications. For example, scientists and engineers are still working on creating materials that can withstand the high speeds and forces involved in firing a railgun projectile.

Overall, while railguns have the potential to be very powerful weapons, there are still many challenges that need to be overcome before they can be used by militaries in a practical way.

They profit off of people not needing a payout . Most people pay money and never use the insurance . Only a few people need a payout , and in that case , they often have to pay a fee ( a deductible ) to get the payout . Let 's say you want a $ 100k insurance policy to protect against your house burning down . The insurance company has figured out that there 's a 1/100 chance of a house burning down during a year . They break even if they charge you $ 1000 / yr . If you pay $ 1200 , that 's a $ 200 profit for them . It 's a risky gamble if you 're only looking at one house but the numbers average out when you 're dealing with 10,000 houses . ... and since they 're dealing with 10,000 customers , there 's * a whole lot of money sitting around * , so they can invest it & make extra profits there . There 's a lot of complications & details , but that 's the gist of it . People pay for insurance policies , never need to use them until death / sickness / accident / weather damage / fraud / stolen goods occur , which then is paid out by policy or by another insurance firm if there is an accused party for damages . In the end , fat cash sitting there until use , constantly replenished by the consumer or other insurance firm .

You are correct . That 's why they 're called " pseudo - random " . URL_0 Edit : I should clarify - I meant that a computer ca n't generate truly random numbers without using external data , such as input from a microphone or a mouse . Computers generate truly random numbers by measuring something in the physical world - entropy - that is very difficult to predict . Even though truly random numbers may appear indistinguishable from numbers that are generated using logical patterns and mathematical algorithms , they are different in that they possess entropy . Entropy is what makes the numbers unpredictable . Computers that do not measure anything from the physical world have no source of entropy that they can use to make the output of their random number generators unpredictable . Given their starting state and the algorithm that they are using , it will always be possible to predict their output . By using unpredictable entropy , an algorithm can perturb its output , thus producing what we refer to as " truly random " numbers . Sources of entropy include hard drive temperatures , timings between mouse and keyboard interrupts , white noise from reverse - biased silicon junctions ... basically anything that can ultimately be traced to quantum decay in the physical world . While linguists can reconstruct ancestor languages , those are just educated guesses as to what people used to say . There are no actual written records of Proto - Indo - European ( or any proto - languages ) . So etymologies of many words in any language are traced back to the ' prestige ' language . Whatever language was spoken by the ' elites ' and ' academics ' is used whenever they needed fancy words . Western European languages use Latin and Greek , Japanese uses Chinese , Hindi uses Sanskrit , Turkish uses Arabic , etc . English uses lots of Latin and Greek roots because European scholars all studied Latin ( and some of them studied Greek ) in the neoclassical era . Academic texts were written in Latin , and it was a big deal when people like Newton or Galileo wrote in their native languages . English also has a lot of Latin roots because the Normans invaded in 1066 and introduced French vocabulary into English . The Norman - French aristocrats called cow meat ' beef ' ( cf . French ' boeuf ' ) , while the farmers who raised the cows called them ' cows ' ( cf . German ' Kuh ' ) . Being that French is a daughter language of Latin , we trace the etymologies of these words back to Latin . Beyond that tracing a word 's etymology is increasingly just plain guesswork . There are n't many scriptures of older languages ( compared to the amount of Latin and Greek writing ) . The big problem is that older languages do n't have many records , if at all . Still , though , linguists have reconstructed proto - Indo - European to a certain extent , which would be the ancestor language of Latin , Greek , Old English , and a bunch of other languages . A lot of more common words can be traced back to what they probably were in PIE , even if dictionaries do n't always print them .

Electricity is generated by magnets moving across wires . Winding the wire up into a coil is better than a straight wire , and having three coils is even better . Arranging three coils around a spinning magnet is the basic principle of the power station 's generator . There will be a transformer box somewhere in your block that has three coils that are connected to the power lines . The coils create a spinning magnet force field , rotating at the same speed as the magnet in the generator . Then there are three more coils , but smaller ones , in the transformer . These secondary coils see the rotating field and it appears just like a spinning magnet to those three smaller coils . So they generate too , but at a lower voltage that is safer for the houses in your street . The three coils can be connected together as a " star " like a Y or as a triangle " Delta " . The power lines connect to the corners of the triangle or the points of the star to give 3 phase power . The centre of the star is also connected and that cable is called " neutral " . Wall sockets are connected to one of the 3 phases and neutral . Big motors in factories can use all three phases . Big office blocks will use different phases for different power outlets to keep a balanced load . The voltage between two of the phases is more than the voltage from phase to neutral because in a star connection ( Y ) that is the voltage across two of the coils . It is n't double though , because the pole of the rotating magnet passes those coils one after another . Meow , think of the electricity that comes into your house as a single wire source . This wire carries a voltage that alternates between positive and negative much like a vibrating guitar string or an ocean wave ( where there is a peak in positive , followed by a trough of negative , sine wave if you know much about graphs ) When this voltage move positive to negative repeatedly it pulls and pushes electrons that generate electricity to power your electronics . Three phase power consists of three of these lines , and think of the three lines as having separate delays so that their peaks and troughs do n't line up . Instead of comparing one line to the neutral ground ( 0V ) for delivering electricity , you have the option to attach an appliance between two of the three " hot " lines . This means that you get to have one side of the appliance be attached to the " peak " while the other to a " trough " , allowing you to have a bigger voltage difference than 120V. This means that you can use a higher voltage coming out of 3 lines of 120V for industrial purposes or etc . Meanwhile , if you do n't need the higher voltage , you can just use one of the lines and let the other two go to other houses . The advantage of this is that you get to have more lines delivering power , which is more reliable and easier to expand while allowing higher voltage in industrial usage . The lines do n't always have to be 120V. I ca n't give a good answer . But thankfully [ this has been asked about 20 times ] ( URL_0 ) . [ This answer ] ( URL_2 ) makes the most sense to me , but like I said there are a number of answers for you to peruse . From the link : --------------------------- Imagine you have 100 doors , and there is a prize behind one . You pick one door and then 98 others are opened . The prize is either behind your door , or the other one that is unopened . Should you switch ? Absolutely , because you had a 1/100 shot of guessing right with your first pick , and a 99/100 chance if you switch . Its the same idea with 3 doors . --------------------- edit : [ here is another link ] ( URL_1 ) which seems to explain it well . I got it from one of the threads that showed up using the search function . There are three different ways to arrange the different prizes . Door [ 1 , 2 , 3 ] 1 . [ Car , Goat , Goat ] 2 . [ Goat , Car , Goat ] 3 . [ Goat , Goat , Car ] Let 's say you pick door number one . If it 's scenario one , then you picked the car . Monty opens either door two or three , and if you switch , you lose . If it 's scenario two , you picked the goat and Monty opens door number three , and if you switch , you will win . If it 's scenario three , then you picked the goat and Monty opens door number two , and if you switch , you will win . So , given the three possible combinations of how the prizes can be ordered , switching makes you win in two of them , making it wise to switch . This analysis is the same whether you pick door number one , two , or three . > specifically one statement : Since the odds of choosing the car on the first round is 1/3 , the odds of losing the game when you switch every time is also 1/3 . Wow ! It took me forever to understand the solution - and you 've just presented the simplest answer I 've ever seen . Let 's play the game - a million times or something like that . Human babies are born much earlier in their development than other animals. This is because human mothers have a much longer pregnancy than most animals. This means that when a human baby is born, it is not fully developed and is not yet able to do things like walk or talk on its own.

In other animals, the mother carries the baby inside her for a shorter amount of time, so the baby is born more fully developed and is able to take care of itself more quickly.

However, the reason that human babies are born earlier in their development is because human brains are very large and complex, and they need more time to grow and develop before they are born. This is why human babies need more time to reach adulthood and become fully functioning.

So, in short, human babies are born more helpless because they have a longer pregnancy and need more time to develop their brains before they are born.

The War on Poverty is a figurehead term , like the War on Drugs , that does n't point to any one piece of legislation or program . It 's a collection of programs each with varying success or failure , that are influenced , helped , or hurt by competing legislation and programs or by political will of what party happens to be influential at any any given time . To a degree , it has worked surprisingly well when it was allowed to . But unfortunately , politics and special interests have wormed their way into the works and programs have been cut or funds diverted to where they do n't do any good . Just looking at the last few years , politicians ( especially Republicans ) have pushed to cut access to clinics ( especially women 's clinics ) , food stamps , welfare , Head Start , PBS , and countless other programs that the poor depend on . Your assertion is false . Does America have the largest economy in the world ? Yes . Would we have that same # 1 economy if social welfare programs from the War on Poverty never existed ? This one is impossible to answer , but I think the answer is clearly no . Imagine after WWII if there had been no social security , no GI Bill , no Interstate highway system , but instead we went with only market based solutions to our problems . Some would be quick to respond that some of these have nothing to do with poverty and I would be quick to point out they have nothing to do with anything the government should be doing by looking at the Constitution . They are ways to get federal money out to improve things that no one else is improving . As others are pointing out , we saw much higher growth early on when democrats were the bigger Santa of the two parties and it declined as conservatives took power in the 80s to the point where growth is almost non existent today . That is not a simple story of one party good , other bad but I think it does point out that the methods to distribute federal dollars used by the GOP , tax cuts and increased defense spending , are not as effective at creating growth . And do n't believe that we have a small government party . That big military spending is in some ways to me a New Reconstruction that is supported by the more Dixiecratic wing of the the GOP and in many ways is not about security but about creating jobs which to me means lots of that GOP backed spending is part of a war on poverty even though most die hard " conservatives " would call me a socialist nutjob . I tend to retort that the modern GOP is about as fiscally conservative as a tuna sandwich . In conclusion , the War on Poverty has helped tens of millions of Americans lead better lives . The Welfare state had problems that needed fixing , but I think the GOP solution of transferring those dollars to defense sector jobs programs has made the Warfare state the new Welfare state and again we are at a point where we need to evaluate spending and eliminate waste because it is having only limited reach by tying it to defense . Handwriting recognition (HWR), also known as handwritten text recognition (HTR), is the ability of a computer to receive and interpret intelligible handwritten input from sources such as paper documents, photographs, touch-screens and other devices. The image of the written text may be sensed "off line" from a piece of paper by optical scanning (optical character recognition) or intelligent word recognition. Alternatively, the movements of the pen tip may be sensed "on line", for example by a pen-based computer screen surface, a generally easier task as there are more clues available. A handwriting recognition system handles formatting, performs correct segmentation into characters, and finds the most plausible words.

Hangovers are caused primarily by dehydration . Have you noticed after your first few beers you just start peeing like a race horse ? From my undergrad biology class , I remember there is a hormone that ethanol inhibits . I looked it up , the hormone is called ADH ( aniti - diuretic hormone ) . This hormone wants to keep water in your body . Ethanol tells this hormone to stop working . This causes your body to release water . The alcohol your drank last night caused you to become dehydrated . You should slowly drink fluids or have chips of ice at first because your stomach could become upset . There is no point in chugging Gatorade because you are likely to throw it up . Just slowly sip water , relax , and keep drinking water until your urine is clear . If you vomit , do not eat anything . Just keep drinking water . When you feel up to it , eat a banana , toast , or something bland . Avoid greasy and spicy foods or high protein foods ( you should n't be in the mood for those anyway ) . They will cause your stomach to produce more acid , which would make potential vomiting very uncomfortable and damaging to your esophagus . Hydrate hydrate hydrate , and be smart . Drink plenty of water before and after drinking . If you want help , drink water . Drink more water . Drink another glass of water . Try not to smoke . Eat something if you wish . When you drink alcohol your body exits Normal Operation mode and shifts into Process Alcohol mode . When you are done drinking , your body has to shift back to Normal Operation mode , except this transition does n't feel good . You can try to stave off a hangover by drinking more booze ( hair of the dog ) but that only delays the moment when you shift back to Normal Operation mode . I 'm not sure a 5 year old should be getting drunk . Then again drunk people often behave like 5 year olds . By peeing their pants ! I have a hangover . The solution is always proper sleep , rehydration and the replacement of salts in your body ( drinking lots of water will cause you to pee out vital salts ) . The alternative way to avoid hangovers is to stay drunk .

Back in first tests , you fuck up a huge chunk of territory . The bikini atoll , white sands , NM , remote middle of nowhere Russia . Then you moved the test underground . You still fuck up a huge chunk of land , but no aerial contamination A lot depends on the yield of the nuclear weapon and how much of the input gets consumed in the process . You need a certain " critical mass " of plutonium or uranium to build a bomb , but once you 've reached that , you want your design to function in such a way that you get the best bang for your buck . That means fissioning the plutonium into lighter elements which typically decay quite quickly . Obviously people live in Hiroshima and nagasaki today , and you can wander round the white sands desert pretty close to whether the very first tests were executed . A nuclear reactor is a bit different since it 's moderated . Might be easiest to think of a bomb that 's only allowed to explode really slowly - you want to burn through your uranium slowly and and use it to heat water . Typically reactors fail because their cooling systems fail and the heat from the reaction ca n't be safely disposed of . While that can lead to an explosion like in chernobyl it 's important to realize that it 's a chemical explosion and not the same as a bomb going off . The explosion just launches the insides of the reactor into the air . When chernobyl blew up it scattered fuel all over the surrounding area . A bomb tries to burn that fuel and scatters the byproducts of doing so . The byproducts tend to be much less dangerous . The essentially fucked up good chunks of territory with nuclear tests . The Bikini atoll has been uninhabited since just after World War II , when it was used for nuclear testing . In the UK , a small island off the coast of Scotland was also rendered uninhabitable for almost 50 years due to it being infected with anthrax in biological warfare tests .

The price of a call option is determined by a number of factors, including the underlying stock price, the strike price of the option, the time remaining until the option expires, the volatility of the underlying stock, and the risk-free interest rate.The most important factor is the difference between the underlying stock price and the strike price of the option, known as the "intrinsic value." If the underlying stock price is higher than the strike price, the option is said to be "in the money," and its intrinsic value is the difference between the two prices. The price of the option will also include a "time value" component, which reflects the probability that the stock price will rise above the strike price before the option expires.The price of a call option can be calculated using a financial model known as the Black-Scholes model. This model takes into account the factors mentioned above to provide a theoretical value for the option.

Sure! Quantum mechanics is a theory in physics that explains how particles behave and interact with each other. It is based on the idea that particles, such as electrons and photons, can also behave like waves, and that their properties can be described using a set of mathematical equations called wave functions. One of the main ideas in quantum mechanics is that particles can exist in multiple states or locations at the same time, a phenomenon known as superposition. This is in contrast to classical mechanics, which describes the behavior of particles based on their positions and velocities. Another important concept in quantum mechanics is the uncertainty principle, which states that it is impossible to know both the precise position and momentum of a particle at the same time. This uncertainty is due to the wave-like nature of particles, which makes it difficult to pin down their exact location. String theory is a theoretical framework that attempts to reconcile quantum mechanics with Einstein's theory of general relativity. It is based on the idea that the fundamental building blocks of the universe are not particles, but rather one-dimensional "strings" that vibrate at different frequencies. These vibrations give rise to the various particles and forces we observe in the universe. One of the main goals of string theory is to provide a single, unified theory that can explain all of the fundamental forces and particles in the universe, including gravity. However, string theory is still a subject of active research and debate, and it is not yet clear if it will ultimately be successful in achieving this goal.

From Wikipedia A frontier market is a type of developing country which is more developed than the least developing countries, but too small to be generally considered an emerging market. The term is an economic term which was coined by International Finance Corporation’s Farida Khambata in 1992. The term is commonly used to describe the equity markets of the smaller and less accessible, but still "investable", countries of the developing world. The frontier, or pre-emerging equity markets are typically pursued by investors seeking high, long-run return potential as well as low correlations with other markets. Some frontier market countries were emerging markets in the past, but have regressed to frontier status. Investopedia has a good comparison on Emerging Vs Frontier While frontier market investments certainly come with some substantial risks, they also may post the kind of returns that emerging markets did during the 1990s and early 2000s. The frontier market contains anywhere from one-fifth to one-third of the world’s population and includes several exponentially growing economies. The other Question    and are they a good option as well? This depends on risk appetite and your current investment profile. If you have already invested in domestic markets with a well diversified portfolio and have also invested in emerging markets, you can then think of expanding your portfolio into these.

It's also important to remember to wear sunscreen when you're outside to protect your skin from the sun's harmful rays. But don't worry, you can still get enough vitamin D even if you're wearing sunscreen. Just make sure to spend a little extra time in the sun if you're using sunscreen.

You don't have to use an agent (broker, as you call it), but it is strongly advised. In some counties lawyers are required, in some not. Check your local requirements. Similarly the escrow companies that usually deal with recording and disbursing of money. You will probably not be able to get a title insurance without using an escrow service (I'm guessing here, but it makes sense to me). You will not be able to secure financing through a bank or a mortgage broker without an escrow company, and it might be hard without an agent. Agents required by law to know all the details of the process, and they can guide you through what to do and what to look into. They have experience reading and understanding the inspection reports, they know what to demand from the seller (disclosures, information, etc), they know how and from where to get the HOA docs and disclosures, and can help you negotiate the price knowing the market information (comparable sales, comparable listings, list vs sales statistics, etc). It is hard to do all that alone, but if you do - you should definitely get a discount over the market price of the property of about 5% (the agents' fees are up to 5% mostly). I bought several properties in California and in other states, and I wouldn't do it without an agent on my side. But if you trust the other side entirely and willing to take the risk of missing a step and having problems later with title, mortgage, insurance or resale, then you can definitely save some money and do it without an agent, and there are people doing that.

The lymphatic system is a network of tissues and organs that help rid the body of toxins, waste, and other unwanted substances. It's kind of like a garbage truck for the body. It has a lot of different parts that work together to keep you healthy.

One important part of the lymphatic system is the lymph nodes. These are small bean-shaped organs that are found all over the body, especially in the neck, armpits, and groin. Lymph nodes contain white blood cells, which help fight infections. When germs or other bad things enter the body, the white blood cells in the lymph nodes attack them to keep you healthy.

Another important part of the lymphatic system is the lymphatic vessels. These are like a network of tubes that carry a clear fluid called lymph throughout the body. Lymph is made up of white blood cells and other substances. It helps to clean up the body by carrying waste and germs away from the tissues and organs.

The lymphatic system also includes the spleen, which is an organ that helps to filter the blood and remove old or damaged red blood cells. The thymus, which is a gland located in the chest, helps to develop the body's immune system. And the tonsils, which are small masses of lymphatic tissue in the back of the throat, help to protect against infections in the mouth and throat.

Overall, the purpose of the lymphatic system is to keep the body clean and healthy by getting rid of unwanted substances.

Easy . Your W-2 was't filled out right and did n't automatically withdraw enough taxes . Then come tax time you owe thousands of dollars because you did nt pay enough during the year . However you did nt realize this was happening and do n't have the money to pay right away . Boom back taxes . The most common way is to owe a small amount ( $ 1 - 3k ) in taxes that you do n't pay ( usually because you meant to get around to it but suck at life and forgot to send in your check or because you spent all your money and ca n't afford to pay the tax bill ) , then the IRS starts charging you interest and penalties on the money you owe . Five years later that small amount you once owed can now be huge . Americans get to choose how many exemptions their employer should assume they 'll have when they do their taxes . We all do it when we start any full - time job . However , lots of people with 1 exemption prefer to tell their employer 2 - 3 exemptions instead . This means they get more money each paycheck but will owe the IRS more money out of pocket at the end . The theory is that the end result is the same , but this way you get to earn interest until Apr 15 on that money instead of the IRS earning interest on it . However , even for young , relatively low income workers , this can easily mean they owe a grand or three in taxes out of pocket at the end of the year . For the rest of us , it 's very easy to end up owing thousands of dollars at the end of each tax year . This is doubly true for anyone who is married . When your employer deducts taxes from your paycheck , they are making an educated guess that you will be in the tax bracket that your yearly income from them would put you in . However , if your spouse makes appreciably more than you , then you owe more ( because they pulled you up into a higher tax bracket ) , but your employer does n't know it . That means you 're going to owe money ( often thousands of dollars ) at the end of the year . The same is true if you just got a big raise mid - way through the year --- half of your checks had taxes deducted at a lower tax bracket , and so the end result will be off . You do n't pay that just once ( or screw up your taxes and miss a big $ 2k adjustment , like you did n't realize that you now make enough that alternative minimum tax applies to you , or you mis - read the instructions and thought you could deduct your home 's new microwave as part of your home office ) , then suddenly you owe money to the IRS in back taxes . Add in penalties and interest , and suddenly this tax burden becomes more than you can afford . [ Edit - fixed my exemption reversal ] I 'm a CPA tax accountant and prior to leaving public accounting for private industry , I had a few cases of this . The most notable was a guy who owned a business ( S Corporation ) and he acted as a financial consultant ( go figure ) . He claimed to me that he had been depressed for several years and despite making hundreds of thousands of dollars over the last 5 years , he had n't filed a single tax return for his business because he was depressed and detached ( but not so much that he could n't rake in $ $ ) . Once we prepared all of his past due tax returns , he owed about $ 300k in just federal taxes excluding penalties and interest , which would 've brought the balance well over half a million dollars . Since he claimed to have depression issues , and his doctor ended up writing a note that we attached to his tax returns when we filed them to abate penalties . I ended up quitting that job before we heard anything back from the IRS , but I guess it could 've gone either way .

When ice cream is made, it is churned while it is freezing to incorporate air and make it smooth and creamy. This is what gives ice cream its soft, fluffy texture. However, when ice cream is left in the freezer for an extended period of time, it can become hard and icy. This is because the water in the ice cream starts to separate from the other ingredients and forms into ice crystals. These ice crystals make the ice cream hard and icy instead of smooth and creamy.

One way to prevent ice cream from becoming hard and icy is to store it in an airtight container. This will help keep the ice cream fresh and prevent it from picking up odors or flavors from other foods in the freezer. Additionally, it is a good idea to let the ice cream sit out at room temperature for a few minutes before scooping it, as this can help make it easier to scoop and give it a softer texture.

It 's generally worth approaching the brain , like the body , as a bunch of sorta - specialized parts that work together but also sometimes fight . The fact that one may have an ingrained fear of death and self - harm from one region may not be enough to prevent another region from overriding it . So , let 's say a person has been socialized in a nation 's schools , told by their loved ones their entire lives that their nation is the best and worth dying for , until it becomes a core belief . Then , they go through rigorous military training which drills habits of acting in unison and not panicking at frightening events . ( This last part is really important , because millennia of experience have taught us that untrained militia will absolutely run for their lives if they get spooked . A big part of military training is replacing that instinct . ) The grenade example is a good one in that it stresses a snap judgment . A three - second fuse does n't give you enough time to weigh pros and cons , or even to consciously process some information . Suicide in the general population is primarily associated with mental illness . While significant suffering is generally involved , suicide remains a generally impulsive act , associated with mental states of great restlessness and drive to action . Intoxication and substance abuse are often involved . Suicide in terminal conditions may be somewhat different and more deliberate , particularly when death is inevitable , but that 's a growing field . If you experience enough pain for an extended period of time , then you can eventually reach a point in your mind in which not being a live is the fastest solution to end that pain . Most people with suicidal ideation do n't truly want to die , that 's why there are often red flag posts , or notes . The cry for help and choosing to end your own life are the solutions that overshadow everything else . Think about how alluring it is to fall asleep after an awful day . When all is said and done , when you slip into bed and close your eyes , everything goes dark . It 's not so much the experience of sleeping that is a reprieve , but having a window where you experience very little or nothing at all . See any of [ these ] ( URL_0 ) . It 's a lot easier to imagine there being more door . It makes the explanation simpler . Try ten , for example . Let 's say you have to choose between doors 1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,9 , and 10 . One has a car behind it , and the others have junk . What are the chances you pick the correct door ? 1 in 10 . Let 's say you choose door 7 . Monty Hall then opens every other door except 2 , and asks if you want to switch . Would you switch ? Yes . Why ? Because the chance that you picked the correct door on the first try is 1/10 , but the chance the other door is correct is 9/10 . If it helps , try imagining more doors . The set up is that at the beginning of the game the chance you originally pick losing door is 1/3 , since only one out of three doors is a winner . But the end of the game , right before you switch , the chance your original door is a loser is still 1/3 . * The question and the paradox is why with only two doors left , why is it your chance of still having a losing door is 1/3 , not 1/2 . * * * The answer to the paradox is that when Monty shows you a door , he does not show you a random door , he purposely shows you a door that gives you zero information * * . At the end of the game , nothing has changed . Your chance of winning has not changed . It was 1/3 at the beginning and it is still 1/3 at the end . To explain why that is true , read on . * * Monty Hall Explained * * You know that when you originally pick a door your chance of winning is only 1/3 and that * at least * on of the doors left has a goat . Knowing that , should you give up your door and pick one of the other two doors ? Who knows , maybe yes maybe no ... the chance of any of the 3 doors is the same , 1/3 . The chance your original pick is a car is 1/3 . Now at this point , Monty * purposely * picks a door with a goat . You know there is one like that so you learn nothing from the fact that he showed you one . In fact , he could have you blindfolded and just told you he opened a door . So him opening a door does nothing . The chance your original door is a winner is still 1/3 after he opens a door just like it was before he opened a door . Since the chance your original door is a winner is 1/3 , that means the chance it is a loser is 2/3 . Well , if you have a 2/3 chance of losing with your door , why keep it when there is only one other door left to pick at the end ? Always switch doors in the * Monty Hall Challenge * .

Gerrymandering is the practice of drawing electoral district boundaries in a way that gives an unfair advantage to a particular political party or group. It is a problem that exists in many countries, not just the United States. In order to address this issue, some countries have put in place independent or nonpartisan bodies to handle the process of drawing electoral district boundaries. These bodies are designed to be free from political influence and to create district boundaries that are fair and representative of the population.

In Canada, for example, the process of redistricting (redrawing electoral district boundaries) is handled by an independent body called the Electoral Boundaries Commission. This commission is made up of judges, academics, and other experts who are appointed by the federal government. The commission is responsible for reviewing the boundaries of electoral districts and making recommendations for changes based on factors such as population changes and community interests.

In Europe, many countries also have independent or nonpartisan bodies that are responsible for drawing electoral district boundaries. In some cases, these bodies are required to follow specific rules or guidelines in order to ensure that the process is fair and free from political influence. For example, in the United Kingdom, the Boundary Commission is required to follow certain rules when redrawing electoral district boundaries, including the need to ensure that the number of voters in each district is as equal as possible.

To explain it like you're five: In some countries, there are special groups of people who make sure that the lines on maps that show where people live and vote are fair and not biased towards any one group. This helps make sure that everyone has an equal say in elections.

If you are upside down on your car loan, it means that you owe more on the loan than the car is worth. This can be a difficult financial situation to be in, but there are a few options that you can consider:Trade in your current car for a less expensive model: If you are able to find a buyer for your current car, you may be able to trade it in for a less expensive model and use the equity from the sale to help pay off some of the remaining balance on your loan.Sell your car and use the proceeds to pay off the loan: If you are able to sell your car for more than you owe on the loan, you can use the proceeds from the sale to pay off the remaining balance and use the remainder to put toward a new car.Roll the remaining balance into a new loan: If you are unable to sell your car or trade it in, you may be able to roll the remaining balance on your current loan into a new loan for a different car. This will allow you to pay off the old loan and start a new one with a new car. However, be aware that this option may result in a higher overall cost due to additional interest charges.It is important to carefully consider all of your options before making a decision. It may also be helpful to speak with a financial advisor or the lender for your current car loan to discuss your options and find a solution that works for you.

When you throw a ball into the air, it follows a curve because of the Earth's gravity. Gravity is a force that pulls objects towards the center of the Earth. Because the Earth is round, the ball doesn't just fall straight down but instead falls towards the ground in an arc. The ball will eventually come back down to the ground because of this force of gravity.

The Earth's rotation doesn't really affect the ball as it goes up and comes down. The Earth's rotation just makes it seem like the ball is moving to the side a little bit as it goes up and comes down.

When you jump into the air, you also follow this curve because of gravity. You will eventually come back down to the ground because of the force of gravity. The Earth's rotation also doesn't really affect you as you jump. It just makes it seem like you are moving to the side a little bit as you jump up and come back down.

Yes, an S corporation (S-corp) can generally write off work and merchandise expenses that it donates to a non-profit organization as charitable contributions. However, there are some rules and limitations that must be followed in order for the donations to be tax-deductible.Charitable contributions made by an S-corp are generally deductible as business expenses, rather than as charitable contributions on the individual tax returns of the shareholders. However, the S-corp's charitable contributions are subject to the same limits and rules that apply to other business deductions.The S-corp must donate to a qualified non-profit organization in order for the donation to be tax-deductible. A qualified organization is generally a non-profit organization that is tax-exempt under Internal Revenue Code section 501(c)(3) and is not a private foundation, unless the foundation is a publicly supported charity.The S-corp's charitable contribution must be made for the purpose of furthering the non-profit organization's charitable, educational, or other exempt purposes, rather than for the benefit of the S-corp or its shareholders.The S-corp can generally deduct the fair market value of the work or merchandise it donates, up to a limit of 50% of its adjusted gross income (AGI) for the tax year. If the S-corp's charitable contributions exceed this limit, the excess can generally be carried forward and deducted in future tax years, subject to the same 50% AGI limit.It's important to note that these rules and limitations are general guidelines, and there may be other factors that affect the S-corp's ability to write off charitable contributions. It's always a good idea to consult with a tax professional or refer to IRS guidelines for more detailed information.

Spicy foods are enjoyable because they contain a chemical called capsaicin, which can be found in things like peppers and chili spices. When we eat these spicy foods, the capsaicin activates special receptors in our mouth and throat that are sensitive to heat and pain. It can also make their mouth feel more alive and give them a rush of energy. Other people enjoy the taste of spicy foods because they add a lot of flavor and depth to the dish.

The original conception of a bank was simply a storehouse for wealth , initially represented by money . Rather than keep all your wealth on you ( robbery ) or at your home ( theft , home invasion robbery ) , you pay for your wealth to be securely guarded by people who 's jobs it is to protect that money . True banking came about when bankers started loaning that money out as loans with interest rates . This was seen as a good idea by depositors , as they would no longer have to pay a fee for their wealth to be guarded . Additionally , banks could now accept more deposits and accounts from more people since every deposit represents float they can use to issue loans ( rather than a cost ) . Bank runs and closures were always an issue of course but far less prevalent than simple robbery . Well you could watch some videos about that on YT . I reccomend extra - history : Money .
